acronyms.usage: DMA is a method of data transfer that allows a device to directly access memory without CPU intervention.
I/O refers to the transfer of data between devices and the CPU.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a CPU directly controlling the flow of data between devices.
Imagine a CPU being notified by an interrupt signal when a device is ready for data transfer.
Imagine a device directly accessing memory without CPU intervention.

analogies.analogy: A person manually controlling a machine to perform a task.
A person being notified by a bell when a task is ready to be performed.
A machine performing a task automatically without human intervention.
